result.output:
For this checkout, the built-in operator entrypoint is [`scripts/start_nusyq.py`](C:\Users\keath\Desktop\Legacy\NuSyQ-Hub\scripts\start_nusyq.py). The fastest help paths are:

- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py help`
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py menu`
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py menu analyze`

The analysis-related commands currently wired are defined in [`scripts/nusyq_actions/menu.py`](C:\Users\keath\Desktop\Legacy\NuSyQ-Hub\scripts\nusyq_actions\menu.py) and [`scripts/nusyq_actions/ai_actions.py`](C:\Users\keath\Desktop\Legacy\NuSyQ-Hub\scripts\nusyq_actions\ai_actions.py). The main ones are:

- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py analyze`
  Runs full system analysis through `AgentTaskRouter.analyze_system()`.
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py analyze <file> [--system=ollama|chatdev|copilot|auto]`
  Routes a specific file to an AI analysis backend.
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py error_report`
  Generates the unified diagnostic report.
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py brief`
  Gives a quick system-state summary.
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py doctor`
  Runs the broader health/diagnostic suite.

Current state already reports these as wired in [`state/reports/current_state.md`](C:\Users\keath\Desktop\Legacy\NuSyQ-Hub\state\reports\current_state.md). I couldn’t execute the help command directly because command execution is blocked by policy in this session, so this is sourced from the repo code and the latest generated state report.

result.output:
Use `python scripts/start_nusyq.py <action>`. The main entrypoint is [scripts/start_nusyq.py](C:/Users/keath/Desktop/Legacy/NuSyQ-Hub/scripts/start_nusyq.py), and the categorized operator guide is [docs/ACTION_MENU_QUICK_REFERENCE.md](C:/Users/keath/Desktop/Legacy/NuSyQ-Hub/docs/ACTION_MENU_QUICK_REFERENCE.md).

Most useful commands:
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py help` for the full command list
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py menu` for the categorized action menu
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py brief` for a quick workspace status
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py doctor` for full diagnostics
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py error_report` for unified error ground truth
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py analyze <file>` for AI analysis
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py review <file>` for code review
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py heal` for non-destructive healing
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py ai_status` for AI/runtime health
- `python scripts/start_nusyq.py work` for an interactive work session

Useful operator phrases from the repo docs:
- “Show me the action menu”
- “Check system health”
- “Generate error report”
- “Review <file>”
- “Debug <error>”
- “Start autonomous cycle”

Current snapshot in [state/reports/current_state.md](C:/Users/keath/Desktop/Legacy/NuSyQ-Hub/state/reports/current_state.md) says that on `2026-03-24` the hub was on `master`, working tree dirty, with `208` lint errors visible in that snapshot and `26` terminal channels active in the ecosystem.

One note: running `python scripts/start_nusyq.py help` directly was blocked in this session, so this summary is based on the launcher source and the repo’s help docs rather than live command output.
